text:
Victor Hu (Lumos Capital): Cheaper than moving everyone on to Zoom, so I had to endure. Yeah, yeah, all good, all good. Well, nice to meet you.

Vrijen Attawar (Careerspan): Great to meet you as well. How's your day going, and where are you calling in from?

Victor Hu: So I'm in North Carolina. I'm just outside of Raleigh in Wake Forest.

Vrijen Attawar: Awesome, awesome, okay.

Victor Hu: Day's going well.

Vrijen Attawar: Great to hear. Not often you hear someone in the VC world not say San Francisco or New York. Occasionally you get a Miami, but that's awesome, and that's a beautiful slice of the planet. I have some good friends in Raleigh, so I visited a few times.

Victor Hu: Yeah, we enjoy down here. So I was in San Francisco for a very long time, almost 15 years before moving here.

[Middle section - transcript truncated in source. Discussion covered: Victor's SF background, VC landscape outside traditional hubs, Careerspan product demo, role decomposition methodology, story-based profile building (3-6 stories), employer use cases for team fit.]

Vrijen Attawar: ...each role down into the individual components. And we also scan for these things in the stories. And then we can tell a candidate, hey, these are the ones that you are missing. Tell a story. Tell a story to fill this gap. And you can actually fill multiple gaps with one story, which is how usually between three to six stories, and, you know, diverse stories, and you've generally completely filled out your profile for basically any job that you could want to apply to. As long as you are being relatively focused in how you apply. But the nuance here is This is one, two, three, four different PM roles that this person has, and each of them... So for folks that are especially particular about the kind of people they work with, that's another potential use case for Careerspan. But if you know anyone in any of those buckets, you know, would appreciate a reference and happy to also be helpful in any way I can.

Victor Hu: Well, part of the reason why I wanted to meet you is because as we look at this category, even the company, for example, that we're about to invest in, and they've given thought to how to utilize or deploy AI coaching as part of their process. And they're, you know, thinking about should they build something, should they partner with someone, et cetera.

Vrijen Attawar: So I wanted to better understand it.

Victor Hu: Yeah. We only had 30 minutes and I have to run to another meeting, but it was great to connect and, you perhaps I'll reach out again and we'll find more time to just learn a little bit more about what you're building and how you're going to market.

Vrijen Attawar: Sounds great.

Victor Hu: Thank you so much, Victor. I'll speak to you soon. Take care.

Vrijen Attawar: Take care.
note: Transcript truncated in middle section - webhook missed, manually imported
